name: Vistry Group PLC
description: A partnerships-focused housebuilder delivering mixed-tenure housing and urban regeneration. Acquired Countryside in Nov 2022. Transitioned fully to partnerships model from Sep 2023. Returns cash via buybacks not dividends.

assumptions.revenueDrivers: Partnerships-led model: 74% partner funded, 26% open market in FY2025. Group ASP £282k (partner funded £246k, open market £391k). 71,501 owned and controlled plots.
assumptions.marginDrivers: Adjusted operating margin 8.5% in FY2025, targeting 12% medium term. ROCE 13.9%, targeting 40% as capital-light partnerships model matures. £828m goodwill from Countryside.
assumptions.risks: £304m building safety provision. South division cost forecasting issues (FY2024). Net debt £144m. Transition execution risk. Partner funded volumes dependent on housing association/local authority funding.
assumptions.catalyst: Capital-light partnerships model should improve ROCE significantly. Net debt reducing (down £37m in FY2025). Share buybacks shrinking share count (321m from 347m). Medium-term margin target of 12%.
